title: salix × ludibunda
intro: Salix × ludibunda, also known as the weeping willow or weeping sallow, is a hybrid plant derived from the white willow (Salix alba) and the goat willow (Salix caprea). The plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and alleviate fever. The medicinal properties of Salix × ludibunda have been used to treat various health conditions, including rheumatism, arthritis, and headaches, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Salix × ludibunda can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for versatile use in herbal remedies.
